column2: Verify user registration (email signup, duplicate check, role assignment), login / logout flows (valid credentials, wrong password, non-existent email), profile management (update name, avatar upload, soft delete), password reset lifecycle, JWT / refresh-token security, and admin user-suspension controls.

column3:
1. Open POST /api/auth/register endpoint.
2. Send JSON payload:
   - Email: "newuser@elysstay.com"
   - Password: "User@123"
   - FullName: "Nguyen Van A"
   - Role: "Tenant"
3. Wait for 201 Created response.
column4:
1. Response status is 201 Created.
2. Response body contains UserId, Email, FullName, Role = Tenant.
3. Database record has Status = Active and PasswordHash is not plaintext.
column5:
1. No account exists with email newuser@elysstay.com.
2. Database is accessible and auth service is running.

column3:
1. Open POST /api/auth/register endpoint.
2. Send JSON with Email = "newuser@elysstay.com" (already registered).
3. Wait for error response.
column4:
1. Response status is 409 Conflict.
2. Error message contains "Email already exists" or unique constraint violation.
3. No duplicate record is created in the database.
column5:
1. Account with email newuser@elysstay.com already exists from Auth-1.
2. Database is accessible.

column3:
1. Open POST /api/auth/register endpoint.
2. Send JSON with Role = "Tenant", Email = "tenant01@elysstay.com".
3. Check the created user record.
column4:
1. Response status is 201 Created.
2. User record Role field = Tenant.
3. User has Tenant-level permissions only (cannot manage properties).
column5:
1. No account with email tenant01@elysstay.com exists.
2. Auth service is running.

column3:
1. Open POST /api/auth/register endpoint.
2. Send JSON with Role = "Owner", Email = "owner01@elysstay.com".
3. Check the created user record.
column4:
1. Response status is 201 Created.
2. User record Role field = Owner.
3. User has Owner-level permissions (property management access).
column5:
1. No account with email owner01@elysstay.com exists.
2. Auth service is running.

column3:
1. Open POST /api/auth/register endpoint.
2. Send JSON with Role = "Staff", Email = "staff01@elysstay.com".
3. Check the created user record.
column4:
1. Response status is 201 Created.
2. User record Role field = Staff.
3. User has Staff-level permissions.
column5:
1. No account with email staff01@elysstay.com exists.
2. Auth service is running.

column3:
1. Open POST /api/auth/login endpoint.
2. Send JSON: Email = "newuser@elysstay.com", Password = "User@123".
3. Read the response body.
column4:
1. Response status is 200 OK.
2. Response body contains a valid JWT accessToken (non-empty string).
3. Response body contains refreshToken.
4. User profile (Id, Email, FullName, Role) is included.
column5:
1. User newuser@elysstay.com is registered and Status = Active.
2. Auth service is running.

column3:
1. Open POST /api/auth/login endpoint.
2. Send JSON: Email = "newuser@elysstay.com", Password = "WrongPass".
3. Read the error response.
column4:
1. Response status is 401 Unauthorized.
2. Error message is "Invalid credentials" or equivalent.
3. No JWT token is issued.
column5:
1. User newuser@elysstay.com is registered.
2. Auth service is running.

column3:
1. Open POST /api/auth/login endpoint.
2. Send JSON: Email = "nobody@elysstay.com", Password = "Any@123".
3. Read the error response.
column4:
1. Response status is 404 Not Found.
2. Error message indicates "User not found" or equivalent.
3. No JWT token is issued.
column5:
1. No account exists with email nobody@elysstay.com.
2. Auth service is running.

column3:
1. Authenticate as newuser@elysstay.com and obtain JWT.
2. Open PUT /api/users/{userId} endpoint.
3. Send JSON: FullName = "Nguyen Van B".
4. Query the database for the updated record.
column4:
1. Response status is 200 OK.
2. Database FullName field = "Nguyen Van B".
3. UpdatedAt timestamp is refreshed to current time.
column5:
1. User newuser@elysstay.com is logged in with valid JWT.
2. Auth service is running.

column3:
1. Authenticate as admin.
2. Open DELETE /api/users/{userId} for target user.
3. Check the database record after deletion.
column4:
1. Response status is 200 OK.
2. Database DeletedAt field is populated with current timestamp.
3. User Status changes to Inactive.
4. User can no longer authenticate.
column5:
1. Target user exists and has no active contracts or leases.
2. Admin account is authenticated.
